title: The Historian's Dilemma
subtitle: Cherry-pick, squash, rebase, purge -- all before dawn

briefing.narrative:
The monastery bell strikes midnight. You've been summoned to the Head Monk's office.

He slides a laptop across the desk. On screen: the messiest feature branch you've ever seen. Five commits, each worse than the last:

1. A good feature commit
2. A WIP commit that should be squashed into #1
3. A commit containing a secret API key
4. Another good feature commit
5. A commit that was meant for the 'hotfix' branch entirely

The Head Monk folds his hands. "Clean. This. Up. Cherry-pick commit 5 to the hotfix branch. Remove the secret. Squash the WIP. Rebase onto main. Merge it clean."

He pauses at the door. "I'll know if you didn't do it properly. The monastery cat tells me everything."

This is the final exam. Every technique you've learned. One branch. One night.
briefing.concept: Real-world branch cleanup often requires combining multiple techniques: cherry-pick to move misplaced commits, reset to remove bad commits, and rebase to update your branch. The key is working methodically -- one operation at a time.
objectives: Cherry-pick the misplaced commit to the hotfix branch
Remove the secret from the feature branch history
Squash the WIP commit into the feature work
Rebase the cleaned feature onto main

args.content:
new feature code
more feature code

new feature code
more feature code
final feature

hints.text: The midnight cat purrs: 'Work methodically. Move what doesn't belong, remove what shouldn't exist, squash what should be together, then rebase and merge. One step at a time.'
